the bug 🐞**

All the examples in the `SciMLSensitivity.jl` Documentation use a user-defined function for the ODEProblem. I need instead to define a parameter-dependent `SciMLOperator` (e.g., a `MatrixOperator`, `AddedOperator`, ...), but it fails.

**Expected behavior**

Returning the correct gradient without errors.

**Minimal Reproducible Example 👇**

```julia
using LinearAlgebra
using OrdinaryDiffEq
using SciMLOperators
using Zygote
using Enzyme
using SciMLSensitivity

const T = Float64
const N = 10
const u0 = ones(T, N)
H_tmp = rand(T, N, N)
const H = H_tmp + H_tmp'

coef(a, u, p, t) = - p[1]


function my_f(γ)
    # U = MatrixOperator(-1im * H - γ * Diagonal(H))
    U = ScalarOperator(one(γ), coef) * MatrixOperator(Diagonal(H))
    tspan = (0.0, 1.0)
    # prob = ODEProblem{true}(U, u0, tspan, [γ], sensealg = InterpolatingAdjoint(autojacvec=false))
    prob = ODEProblem{true}(U, u0, tspan, [γ])
    sol = solve(prob, Tsit5())
    return real(sol.u[end][end])
end

my_f(1.9) # 0.25621142049273665
```

**Error & Stacktrace ⚠️**

#### Zygote Error

Zygote.gradient(my_f, 1.9)

Where the struct contains a `Float64`, but during the differentiation it is trying to convert its field `val` to a `Dual` number, thus changing the type of the structure.

I found a possible partial fix for the out-of-place case. If I consider the out-of-place ODEProblem `prob = ODEProblem{false}(U, u0, tspan, [γ])`, and I update of the `ScalarOperator` is updated in the out-of-place case

```julia
function SciMLOperators.update_coefficients(L::ScalarOperator, u, p, t; kwargs...)
  return ScalarOperator(L.update_func(L.val, u, p, t; kwargs...), L.update_func)
end
```

instead of the current implementation

```julia
function update_coefficients!(L::ScalarOperator, u, p, t; kwargs...)
    L.val = L.update_func(L.val, u, p, t; kwargs...)
    nothing
end

function update_coefficients(L::ScalarOperator, u, p, t; kwargs...)
    update_coefficients!(L, u, p, t; kwargs...)
    L
end
```

Then it works

```julia
Zygote.gradient(my_f, 1.9) # (-0.17161488226273966,)
```

However, it still fails for the Enzyme case and for the in-place version of Zygote (which would be much more efficient I guess).

I will make a PR to SciMLOperators.jl for fix at least this case.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLSensitivity.jl/issues/comments/2451989321/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

the bug 🐞**

I defined a very simple struct just to test the SciMLStructures.jl package, but it fails. I tried with the first example in the SciMLSensitivity.jl Documentation, but using a custom struct instead of a vector as parameters.

**Expected behavior**

Returning the correct gradient.

**Minimal Reproducible Example 👇**

```julia
using OrdinaryDiffEq
using Zygote
using SciMLSensitivity
```

#### Working example from SciMLSensitivity.jl
```julia
function lotka_volterra!(du, u, p, t)
    du[1] = dx = p[1] * u[1] - p[2] * u[1] * u[2]
    du[2] = dy = -p[3] * u[2] + p[4] * u[1] * u[2]
end
p = [1.5, 1.0, 3.0, 1.0];
u0 = [1.0; 1.0];
prob = ODEProblem(lotka_volterra!, u0, (0.0, 10.0), p)
sol = solve(prob, Tsit5(), reltol = 1e-6, abstol = 1e-6)

function sum_of_solution(u0, p)
    _prob = remake(prob, u0 = u0, p = p)
    sum(solve(_prob, Tsit5(), reltol = 1e-6, abstol = 1e-6, saveat = 0.1))
end
du01, dp1 = Zygote.gradient(sum_of_solution, u0, p) # OK
```

#### Not Working example with custom structure and SciMLStructures.jl

Struct definition
```julia
struct MyParameters{T}
  params::T
end

Base.length(p::MyParameters) = length(p.params)

function Base.getproperty(obj::MyParameters, field::Symbol)
  if field ∈ fieldnames(typeof(obj))
      getfield(obj, field)
  elseif field ∈ fieldnames(typeof(obj.params))
      getfield(obj.params, field)
  else
      throw(KeyError("Field $field not found in MyParameters or params."))
  end
end
```

SciMLStructures.jl methods definition

```julia
import SciMLStructures: isscimlstructure, ismutablescimlstructure, hasportion, canonicalize, replace, Tunable

isscimlstructure(::MyParameters) = true

ismutablescimlstructure(::MyParameters) = false

hasportion(::Tunable, ::MyParameters) = true

function canonicalize(::Tunable, p::MyParameters)
  buffer = isempty(p.params) ? Float64[] : collect(values(p.params)) 

  repack = let p = p
    function repack(newbuffer)
      replace(Tunable(), p, newbuffer)
    end
  end
  
  return buffer, repack, false
end

function replace(::Tunable, p::MyParameters, newbuffer)
  @assert length(newbuffer) == length(p.params)
  new_params = NamedTuple{keys(p.params)}(Tuple(newbuffer))
  return MyParameters(new_params)
end
```

Function definition
```julia
function lotka_volterra!(du, u, p, t)
  du[1] = dx = p.p1 * u[1] - p.p2 * u[1] * u[2]
  du[2] = dy = -p.p3 * u[2] + p.p4 * u[1] * u[2]
end


function my_f(u0, p)
  param = MyParameters((p1 = p[1], p2 = p[2], p3 = p[3], p4 = p[4],))
  prob = ODEProblem(lotka_volterra!, u0, (0.0, 10.0), param)
  sol = solve(prob, Tsit5(), reltol = 1e-6, abstol = 1e-6)
  return sum(sol.u[end])
end

my_f(u0, p) # OK
```

Gradient calculation
```julia
Zygote.gradient(my_f, u0, p) # ERROR
```

Also with Enzyme.jl
```julia
using Enzyme

du0 = similar(u0)
dp = similar(p)

autodiff(Reverse, my_f, Duplicated(u0, du0), Duplicated(p, dp) # ERROR
```

the bug 🐞**

I defined a very simple struct just to test the SciMLStructures.jl package, but it fails. I tried with the first example in the SciMLSensitivity.jl Documentation, but using a custom struct instead of a vector as parameters.

**Expected behavior**

Returning the correct gradient.

**Minimal Reproducible Example 👇**

```julia
using OrdinaryDiffEq
using Zygote
using SciMLSensitivity
```

#### Working example from SciMLSensitivity.jl
```julia
function lotka_volterra!(du, u, p, t)
    du[1] = dx = p[1] * u[1] - p[2] * u[1] * u[2]
    du[2] = dy = -p[3] * u[2] + p[4] * u[1] * u[2]
end
p = [1.5, 1.0, 3.0, 1.0];
u0 = [1.0; 1.0];
prob = ODEProblem(lotka_volterra!, u0, (0.0, 10.0), p)
sol = solve(prob, Tsit5(), reltol = 1e-6, abstol = 1e-6)

function sum_of_solution(u0, p)
    _prob = remake(prob, u0 = u0, p = p)
    sum(solve(_prob, Tsit5(), reltol = 1e-6, abstol = 1e-6, saveat = 0.1))
end
du01, dp1 = Zygote.gradient(sum_of_solution, u0, p) # OK
```

#### Not Working example with custom structure and SciMLStructures.jl

Struct definition
```julia
struct MyParameters{T}
  params::T
end

Base.length(p::MyParameters) = length(p.params)

function Base.getproperty(obj::MyParameters, field::Symbol)
  if field ∈ fieldnames(typeof(obj))
      getfield(obj, field)
  elseif field ∈ fieldnames(typeof(obj.params))
      getfield(obj.params, field)
  else
      throw(KeyError("Field $field not found in MyParameters or params."))
  end
end
```

SciMLStructures.jl methods definition

```julia
import SciMLStructures: isscimlstructure, ismutablescimlstructure, hasportion, canonicalize, replace, Tunable

isscimlstructure(::MyParameters) = true

ismutablescimlstructure(::MyParameters) = false

hasportion(::Tunable, ::MyParameters) = true

function canonicalize(::Tunable, p::MyParameters)
  buffer = isempty(p.params) ? Float64[] : collect(values(p.params)) 

  repack = let p = p
    function repack(newbuffer)
      replace(Tunable(), p, newbuffer)
    end
  end
  
  return buffer, repack, false
end

function replace(::Tunable, p::MyParameters, newbuffer)
  @assert length(newbuffer) == length(p.params)
  new_params = NamedTuple{keys(p.params)}(Tuple(newbuffer))
  return MyParameters(new_params)
end
```

Function definition
```julia
function lotka_volterra!(du, u, p, t)
  du[1] = dx = p.p1 * u[1] - p.p2 * u[1] * u[2]
  du[2] = dy = -p.p3 * u[2] + p.p4 * u[1] * u[2]
end


function my_f(u0, p)
  param = MyParameters((p1 = p[1], p2 = p[2], p3 = p[3], p4 = p[4],))
  prob = ODEProblem(lotka_volterra!, u0, (0.0, 10.0), param)
  sol = solve(prob, Tsit5(), reltol = 1e-6, abstol = 1e-6)
  return sum(sol.u[end])
end

my_f(u0, p) # OK
```

Gradient calculation
```julia
Zygote.gradient(my_f, u0, p) # ERROR
```

Also with Enzyme.jl
```julia
using Enzyme

du0 = similar(u0)
dp = similar(p)

autodiff(Reverse, my_f, Duplicated(u0, du0), Duplicated(p, dp) # ERROR
```

the bug 🐞**

The calculation of the gradient on a ODE of `Float64` type works when using params as both `Vector` or a custom `struct` (using SciMLStructures.jl). However, it fails when I simply change the type of the ODE to `ComplexF64`.

It seems that, in the `CompleF64` case, it converts the parameters to a `Vector`. But they are a custom `struct`, so `p.p1` doesn't work.

It works when using a `Vector` instead of a custom `struct`.

**Expected behavior**

Returning the correct gradient as in the `Float64` or as in the `ComplexF64` case with `Vector` parameters.

**Minimal Reproducible Example 👇**

```julia
using OrdinaryDiffEq
using Zygote
using SciMLSensitivity
```

### Definition of the custom struct

```julia
struct MyParameters{T}
  params::T
end

Base.length(p::MyParameters) = length(p.params)

function Base.getproperty(obj::MyParameters, field::Symbol)
  if field ∈ fieldnames(typeof(obj))
      getfield(obj, field)
  elseif field ∈ fieldnames(typeof(obj.params))
      getfield(obj.params, field)
  else
      throw(KeyError("Field $field not found in MyParameters or params."))
  end
end

import SciMLStructures: isscimlstructure, ismutablescimlstructure, hasportion, canonicalize, replace, Tunable

isscimlstructure(::MyParameters) = true

ismutablescimlstructure(::MyParameters) = false

hasportion(::Tunable, ::MyParameters) = true

function canonicalize(::Tunable, p::MyParameters)
  buffer = isempty(p.params) ? Float64[] : collect(values(p.params)) 

  repack = let p = p
    function repack(newbuffer)
      replace(Tunable(), p, newbuffer)
    end
  end
  
  return buffer, repack, false
end

function replace(::Tunable, p::MyParameters, newbuffer)
  @assert length(newbuffer) == length(p.params)
  new_params = NamedTuple{keys(p.params)}(Tuple(newbuffer))
  return MyParameters(new_params)
end
```

### ODE Problem

```julia
const T = ComplexF64

function lotka_volterra(u, p, t)
  dx = p.p1 * u[1] - p.p2 * u[1] * u[2]
  dy = -p.p3 * u[2] + p.p4 * u[1] * u[2]

  return [dx, dy]
end

function my_f(p)
  u0 = T[1.0, 1.0]
  param = MyParameters((p1 = p[1], p2 = p[2], p3 = p[3], p4 = p[4],))
  prob = ODEProblem{false}(lotka_volterra, u0, (0.0, 10.0), param)
  sol = solve(prob, Tsit5(), reltol = 1e-6, abstol = 1e-6)
  return sum(real, sol.u[end])
end

p = rand(4)
my_f(p) # OK
```

### Gradient Calculation (fails)

```julia
Zygote.gradient(my_f, p)
```

the bug 🐞**

The gradient returns a null vector when computing the gradient using complex ComponentArrays as Parameters.

**Expected behavior**

A non-null gradient result.

**Minimal Reproducible Example 👇**

```julia
using OrdinaryDiffEq
using Zygote
using SciMLSensitivity
using ComponentArrays

##

function lotka_volterra(u, p, t)
  dx = p[1] * u[1] - p[2] * u[1] * u[2]
  dy = -p[3] * u[2] + p[4] * u[1] * u[2]

  return [dx, dy]
end

function my_f(p)
  u0 = ComplexF64[1.0, 1.0]

  param = p
  # param = ComponentArray((p1 = p[1], p2 = p[2], p3 = p[3], p4 = p[4],))

  prob = ODEProblem{false}(lotka_volterra, u0, (0.0, 10.0), param)
  sol = solve(prob, Tsit5(), reltol = 1e-6, abstol = 1e-6)
  return sum(real, sol.u[end])
end

p = [0.5, 0.6, 0.7, 0.8]
my_f(p)     # 1.9740765358083163

##

Zygote.gradient(my_f, p)   # ([0.34232539073366874, 0.4509928030672796, 1.3335914824686044, -0.7728475749882253],)

##
```

But if I use `param = ComponentArray((p1 = p[1], p2 = p[2], p3 = p[3], p4 = p[4],))` instead, I get a null vector

```julia
Zygote.gradient(my_f, p)   # ([0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0],)
```

The same doesn't happen for a `Float64` initial vector.

the bug 🐞**

The calculation of the gradient on a ODE of `Float64` type works when using params as both `Vector` or a custom `struct` (using SciMLStructures.jl). However, it fails when I simply change the type of the ODE to `ComplexF64`.

It seems that, in the `CompleF64` case, it converts the parameters to a `Vector`. But they are a custom `struct`, so `p.p1` doesn't work.

It works when using a `Vector` instead of a custom `struct`.

**Expected behavior**

Returning the correct gradient as in the `Float64` or as in the `ComplexF64` case with `Vector` parameters.

**Minimal Reproducible Example 👇**

```julia
using OrdinaryDiffEq
using Zygote
using SciMLSensitivity
```

### Definition of the custom struct

```julia
struct MyParameters{T}
  params::T
end

Base.length(p::MyParameters) = length(p.params)

function Base.getproperty(obj::MyParameters, field::Symbol)
  if field ∈ fieldnames(typeof(obj))
      getfield(obj, field)
  elseif field ∈ fieldnames(typeof(obj.params))
      getfield(obj.params, field)
  else
      throw(KeyError("Field $field not found in MyParameters or params."))
  end
end

import SciMLStructures: isscimlstructure, ismutablescimlstructure, hasportion, canonicalize, replace, Tunable

isscimlstructure(::MyParameters) = true

ismutablescimlstructure(::MyParameters) = false

hasportion(::Tunable, ::MyParameters) = true

function canonicalize(::Tunable, p::MyParameters)
  buffer = isempty(p.params) ? Float64[] : collect(values(p.params)) 

  repack = let p = p
    function repack(newbuffer)
      replace(Tunable(), p, newbuffer)
    end
  end
  
  return buffer, repack, false
end

function replace(::Tunable, p::MyParameters, newbuffer)
  @assert length(newbuffer) == length(p.params)
  new_params = NamedTuple{keys(p.params)}(Tuple(newbuffer))
  return MyParameters(new_params)
end
```

### ODE Problem

```julia
const T = ComplexF64

function lotka_volterra(u, p, t)
  dx = p.p1 * u[1] - p.p2 * u[1] * u[2]
  dy = -p.p3 * u[2] + p.p4 * u[1] * u[2]

  return [dx, dy]
end

function my_f(p)
  u0 = T[1.0, 1.0]
  param = MyParameters((p1 = p[1], p2 = p[2], p3 = p[3], p4 = p[4],))
  prob = ODEProblem{false}(lotka_volterra, u0, (0.0, 10.0), param)
  sol = solve(prob, Tsit5(), reltol = 1e-6, abstol = 1e-6)
  return sum(real, sol.u[end])
end

p = rand(4)
my_f(p) # OK
```

### Gradient Calculation (fails)

```julia
Zygote.gradient(my_f, p)
```

the bug 🐞**

All the examples in the `SciMLSensitivity.jl` Documentation use a user-defined function for the ODEProblem. I need instead to define a parameter-dependent `SciMLOperator` (e.g., a `MatrixOperator`, `AddedOperator`, ...), but it fails.

**Expected behavior**

Returning the correct gradient without errors.

**Minimal Reproducible Example 👇**

```julia
using LinearAlgebra
using OrdinaryDiffEq
using SciMLOperators
using Zygote
using Enzyme
using SciMLSensitivity

const T = Float64
const N = 10
const u0 = ones(T, N)
H_tmp = rand(T, N, N)
const H = H_tmp + H_tmp'

coef(a, u, p, t) = - p[1]


function my_f(γ)
    # U = MatrixOperator(-1im * H - γ * Diagonal(H))
    U = ScalarOperator(one(γ), coef) * MatrixOperator(Diagonal(H))
    tspan = (0.0, 1.0)
    # prob = ODEProblem{true}(U, u0, tspan, [γ], sensealg = InterpolatingAdjoint(autojacvec=false))
    prob = ODEProblem{true}(U, u0, tspan, [γ])
    sol = solve(prob, Tsit5())
    return real(sol.u[end][end])
end

my_f(1.9) # 0.25621142049273665
```

**Error & Stacktrace ⚠️**

```julia
using LinearAlgebra
using SparseArrays
using OrdinaryDiffEq
using SciMLOperators
using Zygote
using Enzyme
using SciMLSensitivity

##

T = ComplexF64
const N = 10
const u0 = ones(T, N)
# H_tmp = rand(T, N, N)
H_tmp = sprand(T, N, N, 0.5)
const H = H_tmp + H_tmp'
const U = ScalarOperator(one(params[1]), coef) * MatrixOperator(Diagonal(H)) + MatrixOperator(Diagonal(H))

coef(a, u, p, t) = - p[1]


function my_f(params)
    tspan = (0.0, 1.0)
    # prob = ODEProblem{true}(U, u0, tspan, [γ], sensealg = InterpolatingAdjoint(autojacvec=false))
    prob = ODEProblem{true}(U, u0, tspan, params)
    sol = solve(prob, Tsit5())
    return real(sol.u[end][end])
end

params = T[1]
my_f(params) # 0.25621142049273665

##

Zygote.gradient(my_f, params)
```

But I get the following  warnings during the differentiation

```julia
┌ Warning: Potential performance improvement omitted. ReverseDiffVJP tried and failed in the automated AD choice algorithm. To show the stack trace, set SciMLSensitivity.STACKTRACE_WITH_VJPWARN[] = true. To turn off this printing, add `verbose = false` to the `solve` call.

┌ Warning: Reverse-Mode AD VJP choices all failed. Falling back to numerical VJPs
└ @ SciMLSensitivity ~/.julia/packages/SciMLSensitivity/ME3jV/src/concrete_solve.jl:207
(ComplexF64[-1.9743163253371472 + 0.0im],)
```

First, how can I know the `sensealg` (and its options) that is automatically chosen?

Then, although I think the problem is related to ReverseDiff.jl, I followed [this page](https://docs.sciml.ai/SciMLSensitivity/stable/faq/#How-do-I-isolate-potential-gradient-issues-and-improve-performance?) and run

```julia
tspan = (0.0, 1.0)
# prob = ODEProblem{true}(U, u0, tspan, [γ], sensealg = InterpolatingAdjoint(autojacvec=false))
prob = ODEProblem{true}(U, u0, tspan, params)

u0 = prob.u0
p = prob.p
tmp2 = Enzyme.make_zero(p)
t = prob.tspan[1]
du = zero(u0)

if DiffEqBase.isinplace(prob)
    _f = prob.f
else
    _f = (du, u, p, t) -> (du .= prob.f(u, p, t); nothing)
end

_tmp6 = Enzyme.make_zero(_f)
tmp3 = zero(u0)
tmp4 = zero(u0)
ytmp = zero(u0)
tmp1 = zero(u0)


Enzyme.autodiff(Enzyme.Reverse, Enzyme.Duplicated(_f, _tmp6),
    Enzyme.Const, Enzyme.Duplicated(tmp3, tmp4),
    Enzyme.Duplicated(ytmp, tmp1),
    Enzyme.Duplicated(p, tmp2),
    Enzyme.Const(t))
```

the bug 🐞**

All the examples in the `SciMLSensitivity.jl` Documentation use a user-defined function for the ODEProblem. I need instead to define a parameter-dependent `SciMLOperator` (e.g., a `MatrixOperator`, `AddedOperator`, ...), but it fails.

**Expected behavior**

Returning the correct gradient without errors.

**Minimal Reproducible Example 👇**

```julia
using LinearAlgebra
using OrdinaryDiffEq
using SciMLOperators
using Zygote
using Enzyme
using SciMLSensitivity

const T = Float64
const N = 10
const u0 = ones(T, N)
H_tmp = rand(T, N, N)
const H = H_tmp + H_tmp'

coef(a, u, p, t) = - p[1]


function my_f(γ)
    # U = MatrixOperator(-1im * H - γ * Diagonal(H))
    U = ScalarOperator(one(γ), coef) * MatrixOperator(Diagonal(H))
    tspan = (0.0, 1.0)
    # prob = ODEProblem{true}(U, u0, tspan, [γ], sensealg = InterpolatingAdjoint(autojacvec=false))
    prob = ODEProblem{true}(U, u0, tspan, [γ])
    sol = solve(prob, Tsit5())
    return real(sol.u[end][end])
end

my_f(1.9) # 0.25621142049273665
```

**Error & Stacktrace ⚠️**

#### Zygote Error

```julia
Zygote.gradient(my_f, 1.9)
```

the bug 🐞**

All the examples in the `SciMLSensitivity.jl` Documentation use a user-defined function for the ODEProblem. I need instead to define a parameter-dependent `SciMLOperator` (e.g., a `MatrixOperator`, `AddedOperator`, ...), but it fails.

**Expected behavior**

Returning the correct gradient without errors.

**Minimal Reproducible Example 👇**

```julia
using LinearAlgebra
using OrdinaryDiffEq
using SciMLOperators
using Zygote
using Enzyme
using SciMLSensitivity

const T = Float64
const N = 10
const u0 = ones(T, N)
H_tmp = rand(T, N, N)
const H = H_tmp + H_tmp'

coef(a, u, p, t) = - p[1]


function my_f(γ)
    # U = MatrixOperator(-1im * H - γ * Diagonal(H))
    U = ScalarOperator(one(γ), coef) * MatrixOperator(Diagonal(H))
    tspan = (0.0, 1.0)
    # prob = ODEProblem{true}(U, u0, tspan, [γ], sensealg = InterpolatingAdjoint(autojacvec=false))
    prob = ODEProblem{true}(U, u0, tspan, [γ])
    sol = solve(prob, Tsit5())
    return real(sol.u[end][end])
end

my_f(1.9) # 0.25621142049273665
```

**Error & Stacktrace ⚠️**

the bug 🐞**

All the examples in the `SciMLSensitivity.jl` Documentation use a user-defined function for the ODEProblem. I need instead to define a parameter-dependent `SciMLOperator` (e.g., a `MatrixOperator`, `AddedOperator`, ...), but it fails.

**Expected behavior**

Returning the correct gradient without errors.

**Minimal Reproducible Example 👇**

```julia
using LinearAlgebra
using OrdinaryDiffEq
using SciMLOperators
using Zygote
using Enzyme
using SciMLSensitivity

const T = Float64
const N = 10
const u0 = ones(T, N)
H_tmp = rand(T, N, N)
const H = H_tmp + H_tmp'

coef(a, u, p, t) = - p[1]


function my_f(γ)
    # U = MatrixOperator(-1im * H - γ * Diagonal(H))
    U = ScalarOperator(one(γ), coef) * MatrixOperator(Diagonal(H))
    tspan = (0.0, 1.0)
    # prob = ODEProblem{true}(U, u0, tspan, [γ], sensealg = InterpolatingAdjoint(autojacvec=false))
    prob = ODEProblem{true}(U, u0, tspan, [γ])
    sol = solve(prob, Tsit5())
    return real(sol.u[end][end])
end

my_f(1.9) # 0.25621142049273665
```

**Error & Stacktrace ⚠️**

#### Zygote Error

```julia
Zygote.gradient(my_f, 1.9)
```

isn't working"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-10-12T14:21:07Z","updated_at":"2024-10-12T14:21:07Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug 🐞**

In my [QuantumToolbox.jl](https://github.com/qutip/QuantumToolbox.jl) package I've implemented the `OperatorSum` struct, which has the same behavior of 

$$
\sum_i \text{ScalarOperator}(c_i) * \text{MatrixOperator} (O_i)
$$

At that time, I wasn't aware of this functionality of this package. However, I saw that the `SciMLOperators` implementation allocates memory when the `MatrixOperator`s are sparse matrices. This doesn't happen in my implementation of `OperatorSum`, which should work in the same way.

**Expected behavior**

No memory allocations.

**Minimal Reproducible Example 👇**

```julia
T = Float64
N = 100
mat_list = [sprand(T, N, N, 0.1) for _ in 1:10]
coef_list = rand(T, 10)

op1 = OperatorSum(coef_list, mat_list)
op2 = mapreduce(i->ScalarOperator(coef_list[i]) * MatrixOperator(mat_list[i]), +, 1:length(coef_list))

v = rand(T, N)
v_cache1 = similar(v)
v_cache2 = similar(v)
op2 = cache_operator(op2, v)

mul!(v_cache1, op1, v);
mul!(v_cache2, op2, v);
v_cache1 ≈ v_cache2

@benchmark mul!($v_cache1, $op1, $v)

@benchmark mul!($v_cache2, $op2, $v)
```

```julia
BenchmarkTools.Trial: 10000 samples with 8 evaluations.

```

## Possible fix

In my implementation, I had similar issues, which were fixed by using the `@inline` macro in front of the `mul!` definition. In this case the macro should be defined in front of [this definition](https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/blob/7ea582b7554d192241196c262a65d58090a5b748/src/basic.jl#L436), but I might be wrong.

However, I have no clue why such macro would remove these allocations.

working"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-10-12T22:50:47Z","updated_at":"2024-10-12T22:50:47Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug 🐞**

When solving a simple ODE using a `ScaledOperator`, I see many memory allocations. If I initialize the operator as a `MatrixOperator`, everything is fine. I think this issue might be related to #246.

**Expected behavior**

Just to have the minimum memory allocations required for an ODE. The memory allocations don't have to scale with the integration time.

**Minimal Reproducible Example 👇**

```julia
T = Float64
N = 100
A = -1 * MatrixOperator(sprand(T, N, N, 0.1))

v = rand(T, N)
A = cache_operator(A, rand(T, N))

tspan = (0.0, 2.0)

prob = ODEProblem{true}(A, v, tspan)
@benchmark solve($prob, Tsit5(), saveat=$[tspan[end]])
```

```julia
BenchmarkTools.Trial: 10000 samples with 1 evaluation.
 Range (min … max):  37.844 μs …  12.317 ms  ┊ GC (min … max): 0.00% … 99.32%
 Time  (median):     43.317 μs               ┊ GC (median):    0.00%
 Time  (mean ± σ):   44.688 μs ± 122.845 μs  ┊ GC (mean ± σ):  2.74% ±  0.99%

    █▃        ▁▁▃▂                                              
  ▂▆███▇▆▆▆▆▆▆█████▇▆▅▄▄▄▃▃▃▃▃▃▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▁▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂ ▃
  37.8 μs         Histogram: frequency by time         64.8 μs <

 Memory estimate: 34.66 KiB, allocs estimate: 425.
```

And with a simple `MatrixOperator` everything is fine

```julia
A = MatrixOperator(-1 * sprand(T, N, N, 0.1))

v = rand(T, N)
A = cache_operator(A, rand(T, N))

tspan = (0.0, 2.0)

prob = ODEProblem{true}(A, v, tspan)
@benchmark solve($prob, Tsit5(), saveat=$[tspan[end]])
```

```julia

As an example

```julia
T = ComplexF64
N = 500
M = 10
# A1 = MatrixOperator(rand(T, N, N))
# A2 = MatrixOperator(rand(T, N, N))
A1 = MatrixOperator(sprand(T, N, N, 5 / N))
A2 = MatrixOperator(sprand(T, N, N, 5 / N))
A3 = MatrixOperator(sprand(T, N, N, 5 / N))

coeff1(a, u, p, t) = sin(p.ω * t)
coeff2(a, u, p, t) = cos(p.ω * t)
coeff3(a, u, p, t) = sin(p.ω * t) * cos(p.ω * t)

c1 = ScalarOperator(rand(T), coeff1)
c2 = ScalarOperator(rand(T), coeff2)
c3 = ScalarOperator(rand(T), coeff3)

H = c1 * A1 + c2 * A2 + c3 * A3

u = rand(T, N);
du = similar(u);
p = (ω=0.1,);
t = 0.1;

@benchmark $H($du, $u, $p, $t)

{"action":"opened","number":255,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/pulls/255","id":2133722621,"node_id":"PR_kwDOHZ-dpc5_LgX9","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/pull/255","diff_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/pull/255.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/pull/255.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/255","number":255,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Remove `Dict{Symbol, Any}` for type stability","user":{"login":"albertomercurio","id":61953577,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYxOTUzNTc3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/61953577?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio","html_url":"https://github.com/albertomercurio","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"The current implementation of `FunctionOperator` requires a `Dict{Symbol,Any}` to handle the keyword arguments. This leads to type instabilities and performance decreases, as in the following simple code:

```julia
using LinearAlgebra
using BenchmarkTools
using SciMLOperators

T = ComplexF64
N = 10

u = rand(T, N)
du = similar(u)
p = (A=rand(T, N, N),)

op = FunctionOperator((du, u, p, t) -> mul!(du, p.A, u), u, p = p, T=T, isinplace=Val(true), outofplace=Val(true), has_mul5=Val(true), isconstant=true)

op(du, u, p, 0.1)

@benchmark $op($du, $u, $p, 0.1)
```

### master branch
```julia-repl
BenchmarkTools.Trial: 10000 samples with 53 evaluations.
 Range (min … max):  864.434 ns … 104.262 μs  ┊ GC (min … max): 0.00% … 98.52%
 Time  (median):     947.340 ns               ┊ GC (median):    0.00%
 Time  (mean ± σ):   996.750 ns ±   1.294 μs  ┊ GC (mean ± σ):  2.57% ±  2.17%

           ▃█                                                    
  ▂▂▃▅▄▃▃▃▅███▄▃▃▃▃▃▃▃▃▄▅▄▄▃▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▁▂▁▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▁▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂ ▃
  864 ns           Histogram: frequency by time         1.34 μs <

 Memory estimate: 560 bytes, allocs estimate: 7.
```

### This PR branch

```julia-repl
BenchmarkTools.Trial: 10000 samples with 989 evaluations.
 Range (min … max):  46.024 ns … 64.056 ns  ┊ GC (min … max): 0.00% … 0.00%
 Time  (median):     48.916 ns              ┊ GC (median):    0.00%
 Time  (mean ± σ):   48.858 ns ±  1.040 ns  ┊ GC (mean ± σ):  0.00% ± 0.00%

   ▃▄▄▂  ▁▁▁▂▂▂▂▁    ▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁ ▁▂▃▆▇█▇▆▅▄▅▅▅▅▅▅▄▄▄▁▁▁▁▁▁▂▂▁▁  ▃
  ▇██████████████████████████████████████████████████████████ █
  46 ns        Histogram: log(frequency) by time      50.9 ns <

 Memory estimate: 0 bytes, allocs estimate: 0.
```

---

To manage to fix this issue, I used `NamedTuple` instead of `Dict{Symbol, Any}`. For this reason, the additional `kwargs` are required when `accepted_kwargs` is provided, in order to initialize the types inside the `NamedTuple`. In principle, the `accepted_kwargs` can be directly obtained from `kwargs` keys, but I left it for consistency with the other functions.

Furthermore, I've extended the methods of the `get_filtered_kwargs`, also supporting `Val` types for `accepted_kwargs`. In this way, the filtering is performed at compile time, otherwise the type of the `NamedTuple` cannot be inferred. {"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/255","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/255/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/255/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/255/events","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/pull/255","id":2599269686,"node_id":"PR_kwDOHZ-dpc5_LgX9","number":255,"title":"Remove `Dict{Symbol, Any}` for type stability in `FunctionOperator`","user":{"login":"albertomercurio","id":61953577,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYxOTUzNTc3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/61953577?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio","html_url":"https://github.com/albertomercurio","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-10-19T15:00:05Z","updated_at":"2024-10-20T12:55:34Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/pulls/255","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/pull/255","diff_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/pull/255.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/pull/255.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"The current implementation of `FunctionOperator` requires a `Dict{Symbol,Any}` to handle the keyword arguments. This leads to type instabilities and performance decreases, as in the following simple code:

```julia
using LinearAlgebra
using BenchmarkTools
using SciMLOperators

T = ComplexF64
N = 10

u = rand(T, N)
du = similar(u)
p = (A=rand(T, N, N),)

op = FunctionOperator((du, u, p, t) -> mul!(du, p.A, u), u, p = p, T=T, isinplace=Val(true), outofplace=Val(true), has_mul5=Val(true), isconstant=true)

op(du, u, p, 0.1)

@benchmark $op($du, $u, $p, 0.1)
```

### master branch
```julia-repl
BenchmarkTools.Trial: 10000 samples with 53 evaluations.
 Range (min … max):  864.434 ns … 104.262 μs  ┊ GC (min … max): 0.00% … 98.52%
 Time  (median):     947.340 ns               ┊ GC (median):    0.00%
 Time  (mean ± σ):   996.750 ns ±   1.294 μs  ┊ GC (mean ± σ):  2.57% ±  2.17%

           ▃█                                                    
  ▂▂▃▅▄▃▃▃▅███▄▃▃▃▃▃▃▃▃▄▅▄▄▃▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▁▂▁▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▁▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂ ▃
  864 ns           Histogram: frequency by time         1.34 μs <

 Memory estimate: 560 bytes, allocs estimate: 7.
```

### This PR branch

```julia-repl
BenchmarkTools.Trial: 10000 samples with 989 evaluations.
 Range (min … max):  46.024 ns … 64.056 ns  ┊ GC (min … max): 0.00% … 0.00%
 Time  (median):     48.916 ns              ┊ GC (median):    0.00%
 Time  (mean ± σ):   48.858 ns ±  1.040 ns  ┊ GC (mean ± σ):  0.00% ± 0.00%

   ▃▄▄▂  ▁▁▁▂▂▂▂▁    ▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁ ▁▂▃▆▇█▇▆▅▄▅▅▅▅▅▅▄▄▄▁▁▁▁▁▁▂▂▁▁  ▃
  ▇██████████████████████████████████████████████████████████ █
  46 ns        Histogram: log(frequency) by time      50.9 ns <

 Memory estimate: 0 bytes, allocs estimate: 0.
```

---

To manage to fix this issue, I used `NamedTuple` instead of `Dict{Symbol, Any}`. For this reason, the additional `kwargs` are required when `accepted_kwargs` is provided, in order to initialize the types inside the `NamedTuple`. In principle, the `accepted_kwargs` can be directly obtained from `kwargs` keys, but I left it for consistency with the other functions.

Furthermore, I've extended the methods of the `get_filtered_kwargs`, also supporting `Val` types for `accepted_kwargs`. In this way, the filtering is performed at compile time, otherwise the type of the `NamedTuple` cannot be inferred. Thus, all the other functions supporting `accepted_kwargs` would also support `Val` types, improving performance.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/255/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/255/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/comments/2424947100","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/pull/255#issuecomment-2424947100","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/255","id":2424947100,"node_id":"IC_kwDOHZ-dpc6QicGc","user":{"login":"albertomercurio","id":61953577,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYxOTUzNTc3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/61953577?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio","html_url":"https://github.com/albertomercurio","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-20T12:55:33Z","updated_at":"2024-10-20T12:55:33Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"I've also made the `mul!` function for the `IdentityOperator`, because the following example was allocating

```julia
T = Float64
N = 10

u = rand(T, N)
du = similar(u)

A = MatrixOperator(rand(T, N, N))

op = T(0.1) * A + T(0.2) * IdentityOperator(N)
# op = T(0.2) * IdentityOperator(N)
# op = T(0.1) * A

@benchmark mul!($du, $op, $u)
```

To manage to fix this issue, I used `NamedTuple` instead of `Dict{Symbol, Any}`. For this reason, the additional `kwargs` are required when `accepted_kwargs` is provided, in order to initialize the types inside the `NamedTuple`. In principle, the `accepted_kwargs` can be directly obtained from `kwargs` keys, but I left it for consistency with the other functions.

Furthermore, I've extended the methods of the `get_filtered_kwargs`, also supporting `Val` types for `accepted_kwargs`. In this way, the filtering is performed at compile time, otherwise the type of the `NamedTuple` cannot be inferred. Thus, all the other functions supporting `accepted_kwargs` would also support `Val` types, improving performance.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/255/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/255/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/comments/2426663516","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/pull/255#issuecomment-2426663516","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/255","id":2426663516,"node_id":"IC_kwDOHZ-dpc6Qo_Jc","user":{"login":"albertomercurio","id":61953577,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYxOTUzNTc3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/61953577?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio","html_url":"https://github.com/albertomercurio","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-21T13:19:28Z","updated_at":"2024-10-21T13:19:28Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Yes, I'm starting to use it due to its dependence of my package QuantumToolbox.jl. The code qualit tests for my package [QuantumToolbox.jl](https://github.com/qutip/QuantumToolbox.jl) are failing since the last 24 hours. This package has OrdinaryDiffEq as a dependency, and the error comes from here because if I set the version to v6.87 everything works.

The strange thing is that the tests were ok also two days ago with the latest version v6.89. So I think that something broke with the last commits in the `lib` folder. Indeed, during the precompilation, I see a lot of warning messages from OrdinaryDIffEq and its lib packages.

**Minimal Reproducible Example 👇**

Just running Aqua on any package with OrdinaryDiffEq ad a dependency

```julia
Aqua.test_all(QuantumToolbox; ambiguities = false, unbound_args = false)
```

I noticed type-instabilities when using `mapslices` on an `Array`. Here there is a MWE

```julia
julia> function test(A)
           return mapslices(sum, A, dims=(3,4))
       end
test (generic function with 1 method)

julia> A = rand(2,2,2,2)
2×2×2×2 Array{Float64, 4}:
[:, :, 1, 1] =
 0.975298  0.351959
 0.159086  0.200224

[:, :, 2, 1] =
 0.78432   0.0186732
 0.495407  0.110606

[:, :, 1, 2] =
 0.922721  0.367518
 0.062931  0.731392

[:, :, 2, 2] =
 0.953113  0.792762
 0.497891  0.652423

julia> test(A)
2×2×1×1 Array{Float64, 4}:
[:, :, 1, 1] =
 3.63545  1.53091
 1.21532  1.69464

julia> @code_warntype test(A)
MethodInstance for test(::Array{Float64, 4})
  from test(A) @ Main REPL[38]:1
Arguments
  #self#::Core.Const(test)
  A::Array{Float64, 4}
Body::Any
1 ─ %1 = Core.tuple(3, 4)::Core.Const((3, 4))
│   %2 = (:dims,)::Core.Const((:dims,))
│   %3 = Core.apply_type(Core.NamedTuple, %2)::Core.Const(NamedTuple{(:dims,)})
│   %4 = Core.tuple(%1)::Core.Const(((3, 4),))
│   %5 = (%3)(%4)::Core.Const((dims = (3, 4),))
│   %6 = Core.kwcall(%5, Main.mapslices, Main.sum, A)::Any
└──      return %6
```

I was wondering first if there is a quick fix for that, like using other functions in a type stable way.

This is my system information

```julia
julia> versioninfo()
Julia Version 1.10.5
Commit 6f3fdf7b362 (2024-08-27 14:19 UTC)
Build Info:
  Official https://julialang.org/ release
Platform Info:

  CPU: 32 × 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-13900KF
  WORD_SIZE: 64
  LIBM: libopenlibm
  LLVM: libLLVM-15.0.7 (ORCJIT, goldmont)
Threads: 8 default, 0 interactive, 4 GC (on 32 virtual cores)
Environment:

I noticed type-instabilities when using `mapslices` on an `Array`. Here there is a MWE

```julia
julia> function test(A)
           return mapslices(sum, A, dims=(3,4))
       end
test (generic function with 1 method)

julia> A = rand(2,2,2,2)
2×2×2×2 Array{Float64, 4}:
[:, :, 1, 1] =
 0.975298  0.351959
 0.159086  0.200224

[:, :, 2, 1] =
 0.78432   0.0186732
 0.495407  0.110606

[:, :, 1, 2] =
 0.922721  0.367518
 0.062931  0.731392

[:, :, 2, 2] =
 0.953113  0.792762
 0.497891  0.652423

julia> test(A)
2×2×1×1 Array{Float64, 4}:
[:, :, 1, 1] =
 3.63545  1.53091
 1.21532  1.69464

julia> @code_warntype test(A)
MethodInstance for test(::Array{Float64, 4})
  from test(A) @ Main REPL[38]:1
Arguments
  #self#::Core.Const(test)
  A::Array{Float64, 4}
Body::Any
1 ─ %1 = Core.tuple(3, 4)::Core.Const((3, 4))
│   %2 = (:dims,)::Core.Const((:dims,))
│   %3 = Core.apply_type(Core.NamedTuple, %2)::Core.Const(NamedTuple{(:dims,)})
│   %4 = Core.tuple(%1)::Core.Const(((3, 4),))
│   %5 = (%3)(%4)::Core.Const((dims = (3, 4),))
│   %6 = Core.kwcall(%5, Main.mapslices, Main.sum, A)::Any
└──      return %6
```

I was wondering first if there is a quick fix for that, like using other functions in a type stable way.

is very useful in LinearAlgebra operations, because it optimizes the performances due to the fact that the compiler knows that it behaves like an identity matrix (possibly multiplied by some factor).

I'm aware that `I(N)` creates a `Diagonal` matrix of type `Bool` with all true in the diagonal. This constructor basically stores a vector inside, which stores all the values of the Diagonal.

In my opinion, it would be very useful to have another construction (let's say `SizedUniformScaling`), which behaves like a `UniformScaling` but additionally stores also the dimension. This is very useful in contexts like Kronecker's product. For example, one can define a method like

`kron(A::Matrix, I::SizedUniformScaling)`

which would be impossible with `UniformScaling`.

I'm currently using the method `kron(A::Matrix, I::UniformScaling)`, but I basically only need to multiply it by an identity, and converting it into a `Diagonal` matrix seems an overkill.

Moreover, this would also be useful for defining methods for other `AbstractMatrix`s, for example for CUDA, Metal, DistributedArrays, and many others.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55922/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55922/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

I was trying several operations with tensors, and I went into several type instabilities. In the following I show you two examples involving  `mapslices`  and `PermutedDimsArray` functions.

```julia
using LinearAlgebra
```

First case
```julia
@code_warntype mapslices(tr, rand(Float64, 4, 4, 4, 4), dims=(3,4))
```
```julia
MethodInstance for Core.kwcall(::@NamedTuple{dims::Tuple{Int64, Int64}}, ::typeof(mapslices), ::typeof(tr), ::Array{Float64, 4})
  from kwcall(::NamedTuple, ::typeof(mapslices), f, A::AbstractArray) @ Base abstractarray.jl:3175
Arguments
  _::Core.Const(Core.kwcall)

  f::Core.Const(LinearAlgebra.tr)
  A::Array{Float64, 4}
Locals
  @_6::Tuple{Int64, Int64}
  dims::Tuple{Int64, Int64}
Body::Any
1 ─       Core.NewvarNode(:(@_6))
│   %2  = Core.isdefined(@_2, :dims)::Core.Const(true)
└──       goto #3 if not %2
2 ─       (@_6 = Core.getfield(@_2, :dims))
└──       goto #4
3 ─       Core.Const(:(Core.UndefKeywordError(:dims)))
└──       Core.Const(:(@_6 = Core.throw(%6)))
4 ┄ %8  = @_6::Tuple{Int64, Int64}
│         (dims = %8)
│   %10 = (:dims,)::Core.Const((:dims,))
│   %11 = Core.apply_type(Core.NamedTuple, %10)::Core.Const(NamedTuple{(:dims,)})
│   %12 = Base.structdiff(@_2, %11)::Core.Const(NamedTuple())
│   %13 = Base.pairs(%12)::Core.Const(Base.Pairs{Symbol, Union{}, Tuple{}, @NamedTuple{}}())
│   %14 = Base.isempty(%13)::Core.Const(true)
└──       goto #6 if not %14
5 ─       goto #7
6 ─       Core.Const(:(Base.kwerr(@_2, @_3, f, A)))
7 ┄ %18 = Base.:(var"#mapslices#199")(dims, @_3, f, A)::Any
└──       return %18
```

Second case
```julia
@code_warntype PermutedDimsArray(rand(Float64, 4, 4, 4, 4), [4,2,3,1])
```

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/DiffEqCallbacks.jl/issues/217","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/DiffEqCallbacks.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/DiffEqCallbacks.jl/issues/217/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/DiffEqCallbacks.jl/issues/217/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/DiffEqCallbacks.jl/issues/217/events","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/DiffEqCallbacks.jl/issues/217","id":2241825833,"node_id":"I_kwDOBKkOHs6Fn4wp","number":217,"title":"Memory allocations (Still) of `PresetTimeCallback` after v3.2.0","user":{"login":"albertomercurio","id":61953577,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYxOTUzNTc3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/61953577?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio","html_url":"https://github.com/albertomercurio","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-04-13T23:58:32Z","updated_at":"2024-04-13T23:58:32Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the example**

Hello, a couple of weeks ago I created issue #211 , showing an increasing of memory allocations for versions larger than v3.2.0.
I noticed that the MWE was not very minimal, in the sense that, by adding the callback, the number of saved states increased a lot. To overcome this, here I propose a very similar working example, where I define the `saveat = [list[end]]` to save the state only once. In this way, I get 1.07 k allocations against the 56 of the versions <3.2.0, a big difference, since it scales also with the length of tlist.

**Minimal Reproducible Example 👇**

```julia
using QuantumToolbox

# Define a simple ODE system
function simple_ode!(du, u, p, t)
    mul!(du, p.A, u, p.α[1], 0)
end

# Initial condition
u0 = rand(ComplexF64, 10)
A = rand(ComplexF64, 10, 10)

# Time span for the solution
tlist = range(0, 10, 1000)
tspan = (tlist[1], tlist[end])

p = (A=A,α=rand(ComplexF64, 2))

# Define the ODE problem
prob = ODEProblem(simple_ode!, u0, tspan, p, saveat=[tlist[end]])

# Callback function to modify the parameter
function change_param!(integrator)
    integrator.p.α[1] = 0.5 # Change the parameter value
end

callback = PresetTimeCallback(tlist, change_param!, save_positions=(false,false))

# Solve the ODE with the callback
sol = solve(prob, Tsit5(), callback=callback);

@time solve(prob, Tsit5(), callback=callback);
 0.000657 seconds (1.07 k allocations: 68.859 KiB)

solve(prob, Tsit5());
@time solve(prob, Tsit5());
0.000063 seconds (48 allocations: 6.250 KiB)
```

# Define a simple ODE system
function simple_ode!(du, u, p, t)
    mul!(du, p.A, u, p.α[1], 0)
end

# Initial condition
u0 = rand(ComplexF64, 10)
A = rand(ComplexF64, 10, 10)

# Time span for the solution
tlist = range(0, 10, 1000)
tspan = (tlist[1], tlist[end])

p = (A=A,α=rand(ComplexF64, 2))

# Define the ODE problem
prob = ODEProblem(simple_ode!, u0, tspan, p, saveat=[tlist[end]])

# Callback function to modify the parameter
function change_param!(integrator)
    integrator.p.α[1] = 0.5 # Change the parameter value
end

callback = PresetTimeCallback(tlist, change_param!, save_positions=(false,false))

# Solve the ODE with the callback
sol = solve(prob, Tsit5(), callback=callback);

@time solve(prob, Tsit5(), callback=callback);
 0.000657 seconds (1.07 k allocations: 68.859 KiB)

solve(prob, Tsit5());
@time solve(prob, Tsit5());
0.000063 seconds (48 allocations: 6.250 KiB)
```

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/DiffEqCallbacks.jl/issues/211","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/DiffEqCallbacks.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/DiffEqCallbacks.jl/issues/211/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/DiffEqCallbacks.jl/issues/211/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/DiffEqCallbacks.jl/issues/211/events","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/DiffEqCallbacks.jl/issues/211","id":2205918906,"node_id":"I_kwDOBKkOHs6De6a6","number":211,"title":"Memory allocations of `PresetTimeCallback` after v3.2.0","user":{"login":"albertomercurio","id":61953577,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYxOTUzNTc3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/61953577?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio","html_url":"https://github.com/albertomercurio","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-03-25T14:39:04Z","updated_at":"2024-03-25T14:39:04Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the example**

I get a lot of memory allocations, never obtained before v3.2.0

**Minimal Reproducible Example 👇**

```julia
# Define a simple ODE system
function simple_ode!(du, u, p, t)
    mul!(du, p.A, u, p.α[1], 0)
end

# Initial condition
u0 = rand(ComplexF64, 10)
A = rand(ComplexF64, 10, 10)

# Time span for the solution
tlist = range(0, 10, 1000)
tspan = (tlist[1], tlist[end])

# Parameters, with the first parameter being modified by the callback
p = (A=A,α=rand(2))

# Define the ODE problem
prob = ODEProblem(simple_ode!, u0, tspan, p)

# Callback function to modify the parameter p[1]
function change_param!(integrator)
    integrator.p.α[1] = 0.5 # Change the parameter value
end

# Define TimePresetCallback to trigger at t = 2.0 and t = 5.0
callback = PresetTimeCallback(tlist, change_param!, save_positions=(false,false))

# Solve the ODE with the callback
sol = solve(prob, Tsit5(), callback=callback);

@time solve(prob, Tsit5(), callback=callback);
0.001125 seconds (26.55 k allocations: 2.361 MiB)

@time solve(prob, Tsit5());
0.000074 seconds (980 allocations: 101.891 KiB)
```

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/DiffEqGPU.jl/issues/183","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/DiffEqGPU.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/DiffEqGPU.jl/issues/183/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/DiffEqGPU.jl/issues/183/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/DiffEqGPU.jl/issues/183/events","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/DiffEqGPU.jl/issues/183","id":1401176772,"node_id":"I_kwDOC4mTVc5ThELE","number":183,"title":"Support for ODE with Matrix-Vector multiplication","user":{"login":"albertomercurio","id":61953577,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYxOTUzNTc3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/61953577?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio","html_url":"https://github.com/albertomercurio","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2022-10-07T13:28:58Z","updated_at":"2022-12-04T18:18:15Z","closed_at":"2022-12-03T11:00:17Z","author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Hello, 

I need to use matrix-vector multiplication inside my ODE problem. This is often used in quantum physics.

The simplest example should be

```julia
using LinearAlgebra
using SparseArrays
using DifferentialEquations
using DiffEqGPU

A = sprand(ComplexF32, 100, 100, 0.1) # Also dense should be ok. But better if sparse.
A += A'
A = -1im * A

t_l = collect(LinRange{Float32}(0, 10, 100))
tspan = (t_l[1], t_l[end])

function dudt!(du,u,p,t)
    A = p[1]
    mul!(du, A, u)
    nothing
end

u0 = normalize(rand(ComplexF32, N))
p = [A]
prob = ODEProblem(dudt!,u0,tspan,p)
prob_func = (prob,i,repeat) -> remake(prob,u0=normalize(rand(ComplexF32, N)))
monteprob = EnsembleProblem(prob, prob_func = prob_func, safetycopy=false)
@time sol = solve(monteprob, Tsit5(), EnsembleGPUArray(), trajectories=10, saveat=t_l)
```

But it doesn't work. The only way to make it work is by defining the matrix-vector multiplication from myslelf, and using only the matrix A as parameter

```julia
function dudt!(du,u,p,t)
    A = p
    @inbounds begin
        for i in 1:size(A, 1)
            sum = 0
            for j in 1:size(A, 2)
                sum += A[i, j] * u[j]
            end
            du[i] = sum
        end
    end
    nothing
end

p = A
```

But it is very slow, and I need to introduce other parameters in the future.

I was told that using `modelingtoolkitize` could be the correct way. However, I don't have any benefit to making `u0` a `CuArray` since it is relatively too small to see the improvements of the GPU.

This is a widespread problem for studying open quantum systems using "Quantum Trajectories". This method is an alternative to the "Liouvilian dynamics", which however needs very large sparse matrices. Thus, quantum trajectories have the advantage of using relatively small matrices (more or less 100x100), and the _disadvantage_ to do multiple trajectories.

I thought that this package could improve the Quantum Trajectories approach, making the parallelization of the trajectories directly in the GPU, but maybe it is too early.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/DiffEqGPU.jl/issues/comments/1336483352/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

I'm interested on using these tools with CUDA Arrays like ComplexF64 dense CuArray or sparse CuSparseMatrix. Following the documentation, it only needs that the array supports the following functions:

- [x]  `Base.eltype(A)`
- [x]  `Base.size(A, dim)`
- [x]  `LinearAlgebra.mul!(y, A, x)`
- [ ]  `LinearAlgebra.opnorm(A, p=Inf)` (I can insert manually this value)
- [x]  `LinearAlgebra.ishermitian(A)`

To prove that this is the following code:
```julia
test = CUSPARSE.CuSparseMatrixCSC(sprand(ComplexF64, 100, 100, 0.01))
if Base.eltype(test) == ComplexF64
    println("OK")
end
if Base.size(test, 2) == 100
    println("OK")
end
dy = CUDA.zeros(ComplexF64, 100)
y = CUDA.rand(ComplexF64, 100)
LinearAlgebra.mul!(dy, test, y)
test2 = Array(test)
dy2 = Array(dy)
y2 = Array(y)
LinearAlgebra.mul!(dy2, test2, y2)
if Array(dy) == dy2
    println("OK")
end
```

But I can't calculate for example the function `expv` that it returns the error

```julia
expv(1.0, test, y, opnorm = opnorm(test2))

Scalar indexing is disallowed.
Invocation of getindex resulted in scalar indexing of a GPU array.
This is typically caused by calling an iterating implementation of a method.
Such implementations *do not* execute on the GPU, but very slowly on the CPU,
and therefore are only permitted from the REPL for prototyping purposes.
If you did intend to index this array, annotate the caller with @allowscalar.
```

The same problem happens for the dense matrix case.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/issues/80/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/issues/80/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"completed"}}

I'm writing a code that resizes the ODE through a DiscreteCallback, and in the meantime, I have also a SavingCallback to save some values.

The minimal working code is
```julia
L = sprand(ComplexF64, 9, 9, 0.9)
u0 = rand(ComplexF64, 9)
t_l = LinRange(0, 100000, 1000)
tspan = (t_l[1], t_l[end])

progr = Progress(length(t_l))

saved_values = SavedValues(Float64, Vector{ComplexF64})
function save_func(u, t, integrator)
    next!(progr)
    ρt = reshape(u, 3, 3)
    rand(ComplexF64,2)
end
cb = SavingCallback(save_func, saved_values, saveat = t_l)

dudt! = (du,u,p,t) -> mul!(du, L, u)
prob = ODEProblem(dudt!, u0, tspan)

integrator = init(prob, Tsit5(), callback = cb1)
step!(integrator)

# Now I want to resize the ODE, in the future this will be implemented through a DiscreteCallback
resize!(integrator, 12)


cannot resize array with shared data

Nice. Is that copy-paste from @Jutho's package?

Yes, but with a bit of improvement.

> Also, could/should we add dispatch rules for the `eigen` function to call this whenever possible and meaningful?

The `hseqr!` function computes the eigenvalues and, optionally, the Schur decomposition. It never gives you the eigenvectors. Thus, I think the correct comparison should be with the `schur` function, which calculates the factorization for a general matrix.

I will do it soon.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1347637004/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

```
using LinearAlgebra, DifferentialEquations, DiffEqGPU
A  = Float32[1. 0  0 -5; 4 -2  4 -3; -4  0  0  1; 5 -2  2  3]
u0 = Array{Float32}(rand(4,2))
tspan = (0.0f0,1.0f0)
function fip(du,u,p,t) 
   mul!(du, A, u)
end
probip = ODEProblem(fip,u0,tspan)
function prob_func(prob, i, repeat)
    prob
end
ensprobip=EnsembleProblem(probip, prob_func=prob_func)
trajs = 123
simgpu = solve(ensprobip, Tsit5(), EnsembleGPUArray(), trajectories=trajs)
```

i ran it on a linux machine with CUDA 9.0 and several Tesla K80 GPUs with compute capability 3.7.  

see also this thread: https://discourse.julialang.org/t/test-diffeqgpu-errors-with-unsupported-call-to-the-julia-runtime/28893/17

with `EnsembleCPUArray` it works fine,  but it fails to compile on GPU with the stack trace:

I also need to use of matrix-vector multiplication. The simplest example should be

```julia
using QuPhys
using LinearAlgebra
using SparseArrays
using DifferentialEquations
using DiffEqGPU

A = sprand(ComplexF32, 100, 100, 0.1) # Also dense should be ok. But better if sparse.
A += A'
A = -1im * A

t_l = collect(LinRange{Float32}(0, 10, 100))
tspan = (t_l[1], t_l[end])

function dudt!(du,u,p,t)
    A = p[1]
    mul!(du, A, u)
    nothing
end

u0 = normalize(rand(ComplexF32, N))
p = [A]
prob = ODEProblem(dudt!,u0,tspan,p)
prob_func = (prob,i,repeat) -> remake(prob,u0=normalize(rand(ComplexF32, N)))
monteprob = EnsembleProblem(prob, prob_func = prob_func, safetycopy=false)
@time sol = solve(monteprob, Tsit5(), EnsembleGPUArray(), trajectories=10, saveat=t_l)
```

But it doesn't work. The only way to make it work is by defining

```julia
function dudt!(du,u,p,t)
    A = p
    @inbounds begin
        for i in 1:size(A, 1)
            sum = 0
            for j in 1:size(A, 2)
                sum += A[i, j] * u[j]
            end
            du[i] = sum
        end
    end
    nothing
end

p = A
```

But it is very slow, and I need to introduce other parameters in the future.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/DiffEqGPU.jl/issues/comments/1269115174/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

similarly to #1248 I created a separate definition of `get_tmp_cache` for the LinearExponential algorithm. Indeed, prior to this change the following code failed

```julia
using OrdinaryDiffEq
using LinearAlgebra
using SparseArrays
A = sprand(ComplexF64, 100, 100, 0.5)
A += A'

t_l = LinRange(0, 1, 100)

saved_values = SavedValues(Float64, Float64)
function save_func(u, t, integrator)
    real(u' * A * u)
end
cb = SavingCallback(save_func, saved_values, saveat = t_l)

u0 = normalize(rand(ComplexF64, 100))
A = DiffEqArrayOperator(-1im * A)
prob = ODEProblem(A, u0, (0, 1.0))
solve(prob, LinearExponential(), dt = t_l[2] - t_l[1], callback = cb);




ERROR: LoadError: type LinearExponential has no field dz

similarly to #1248 I created a separate definition of `get_tmp_cache` for the LinearExponential algorithm. Indeed, prior to this change the following code failed

```julia
using OrdinaryDiffEq
using LinearAlgebra
using SparseArrays
A = sprand(ComplexF64, 100, 100, 0.5)
A += A'

t_l = LinRange(0, 1, 100)

saved_values = SavedValues(Float64, Float64)
function save_func(u, t, integrator)
    real(u' * A * u)
end
cb = SavingCallback(save_func, saved_values, saveat = t_l)

u0 = normalize(rand(ComplexF64, 100))
A = DiffEqArrayOperator(-1im * A)
prob = ODEProblem(A, u0, (0, 1.0))
solve(prob, LinearExponential(), dt = t_l[2] - t_l[1], callback = cb);




ERROR: LoadError: type LinearExponential has no field dz
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/OrdinaryDiffEq.jl/issues/1777/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/OrdinaryDiffEq.jl/issues/1777/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/OrdinaryDiffEq.jl/issues/comments/1272497381","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/OrdinaryDiffEq.jl/pull/1777#issuecomment-1272497381","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/OrdinaryDiffEq.jl/issues/1777","id":1272497381,"node_id":"IC_kwDOBEVPe85L2MTl","user":{"login":"albertomercurio","id":61953577,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYxOTUzNTc3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/61953577?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio","html_url":"https://github.com/albertomercurio","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-10-09T09:19:12Z","updated_at":"2022-10-09T09:19:12Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Ok, {"action":"opened","number":101,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/101","id":1090090707,"node_id":"PR_kwDOCJGC9s5A-XbT","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pull/101","diff_url":"https://github.com/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pull/101.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pull/101.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/issues/101","number":101,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"GPU support for expv and expv_timestep","user":{"login":"albertomercurio","id":61953577,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYxOTUzNTc3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/61953577?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio","html_url":"https://github.com/albertomercurio","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Hello,

by changing only the syntax of several lines I implemented the GPU support for the `expv` and `expv_timestep` functions. The following code works now

```julia
using LinearAlgebra
using SparseArrays
using ExponentialUtilities
using BenchmarkTools
using CUDA
using CUDA.CUSPARSE
CUDA.allowscalar(false)
##
n = 70000
A = sprand(ComplexF64, n, n, 10 / n)
A = triu(A, 1) + sprand(ComplexF64, n, n, 1 / n)
b = rand(ComplexF64, n)

A = SparseMatrixCSC(A)
b = Array(b)

t = 0.1
ts = Array(LinRange(0, 1, 300))

expv(t, A, b)
@btime expv_timestep(ts, A, b)

A = CuSparseMatrixCSR(A)
b = CuArray(b)

expv(t, A, b)

@btime expv_timestep(ts, A, b)
```","created_at":"2022-10-18T00:45:52Z","updated_at":"2022-10-18T00:45:52Z","closed_at":null,"merged_at":null,"merge_commit_sha":null,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"requested_reviewers":[],"requested_teams":[],"labels":[],"milestone":null,"draft":false,"commits_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/101/commits","review_comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/101/comments","review_comment_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/comments{/number}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/issues/101/comments","statuses_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/statuses/e91873dc4a02aabce2ac27314cc6b93fb5ad7dd3","head":{"label":"albertomercurio:master","ref":"master","sha":"e91873dc4a02aabce2ac27314cc6b93fb5ad7dd3","user":{"login":"albertomercurio","id":61953577,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYxOTUzNTc3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/61953577?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio","html_url":"https://github.com/albertomercurio","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"repo":{"id":540984174,"node_id":"R_kgDOID7Dbg","name":"ExponentialUtilities.jl","full_name":"albertomercurio/Exponent by changing only the syntax of several lines I implemented the GPU support for the `expv` and `expv_timestep` functions. The following code works now

```julia
using LinearAlgebra
using SparseArrays
using ExponentialUtilities
using BenchmarkTools
using CUDA
using CUDA.CUSPARSE
CUDA.allowscalar(false)
##
n = 70000
A = sprand(ComplexF64, n, n, 10 / n)
A = triu(A, 1) + sprand(ComplexF64, n, n, 1 / n)
b = rand(ComplexF64, n)

t = 0.1
ts = Array(LinRange(0, 1, 300))

expv(t, A, b)
@btime expv_timestep(ts, A, b)

A = CuSparseMatrixCSR(A)
b = CuArray(b)

expv(t, A, b)

@btime expv_timestep(ts, A, b)
```","created_at":"2022-10-18T00:45:52Z","updated_at":"2022-10-18T08:56:46Z","closed_at":null,"merged_at":null,"merge_commit_sha":"ffbbdba1bb256b76ace9fff65a94ed782610f6e9","assignee":null,"assignees":[],"requested_reviewers":[],"requested_teams":[],"labels":[],"milestone":null,"draft":false,"commits_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/101/commits","review_comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/101/comments","review_comment_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/comments{/number}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/issues/101/comments","statuses_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/statuses/e91873dc4a02aabce2ac27314cc6b93fb5ad7dd3","head":{"label":"albertomercurio:master","ref":"master","sha":"e91873dc4a02aabce2ac27314cc6b93fb5ad7dd3","user":{"login":"albertomercurio","id":61953577,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYxOTUzNTc3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/61953577?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio","html_url":"https://github.com/albertomercurio","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"repo":{"id":540984174,"node_id":"R_kgDOID7Dbg","name":"ExponentialUtilities.jl" by changing only the syntax of several lines I implemented the GPU support for the `expv` and `expv_timestep` functions. The following code works now

```julia
using LinearAlgebra
using SparseArrays
using ExponentialUtilities
using BenchmarkTools
using CUDA
using CUDA.CUSPARSE
CUDA.allowscalar(false)
##
n = 70000
A = sprand(ComplexF64, n, n, 10 / n)
A = triu(A, 1) + sprand(ComplexF64, n, n, 1 / n)
b = rand(ComplexF64, n)

t = 0.1
ts = Array(LinRange(0, 1, 300))

expv(t, A, b)
@btime expv_timestep(ts, A, b)

A = CuSparseMatrixCSR(A)
b = CuArray(b)

expv(t, A, b)

@btime expv_timestep(ts, A, b)
```","created_at":"2022-10-18T00:45:52Z","updated_at":"2022-10-18T08:56:46Z","closed_at":null,"merged_at":null,"merge_commit_sha":"ffbbdba1bb256b76ace9fff65a94ed782610f6e9","assignee":null,"assignees":[],"requested_reviewers":[],"requested_teams":[],"labels":[],"milestone":null,"draft":false,"commits_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/101/commits","review_comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/101/comments","review_comment_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/comments{/number}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/issues/101/comments","statuses_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/statuses/e91873dc4a02aabce2ac27314cc6b93fb5ad7dd3","head":{"label":"albertomercurio:master","ref":"master","sha":"e91873dc4a02aabce2ac27314cc6b93fb5ad7dd3","user":{"login":"albertomercurio","id":61953577,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYxOTUzNTc3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/61953577?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio","html_url":"https://github.com/albertomercurio","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"repo":{"id":540984174,"node_id":"R_kgDOID7Dbg","name":"ExponentialUtilities.jl" by changing only the syntax of several lines I implemented the GPU support for the `expv` and `expv_timestep` functions. The following code works now

```julia
using LinearAlgebra
using SparseArrays
using ExponentialUtilities
using BenchmarkTools
using CUDA
using CUDA.CUSPARSE
CUDA.allowscalar(false)
##
n = 70000
A = sprand(ComplexF64, n, n, 10 / n)
A = triu(A, 1) + sprand(ComplexF64, n, n, 1 / n)
b = rand(ComplexF64, n)

t = 0.1
ts = Array(LinRange(0, 1, 300))

expv(t, A, b)
@btime expv_timestep(ts, A, b)

A = CuSparseMatrixCSR(A)
b = CuArray(b)

expv(t, A, b)

@btime expv_timestep(ts, A, b)
```","created_at":"2022-10-18T00:45:52Z","updated_at":"2022-10-18T08:58:36Z","closed_at":null,"merged_at":null,"merge_commit_sha":"ffbbdba1bb256b76ace9fff65a94ed782610f6e9","assignee":null,"assignees":[],"requested_reviewers":[],"requested_teams":[],"labels":[],"milestone":null,"draft":false,"commits_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/101/commits","review_comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/101/comments","review_comment_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/comments{/number}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/issues/101/comments","statuses_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/statuses/e91873dc4a02aabce2ac27314cc6b93fb5ad7dd3","head":{"label":"albertomercurio:master","ref":"master","sha":"e91873dc4a02aabce2ac27314cc6b93fb5ad7dd3","user":{"login":"albertomercurio","id":61953577,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYxOTUzNTc3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/61953577?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio","html_url":"https://github.com/albertomercurio","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"repo":{"id":540984174,"node_id":"R_kgDOID7Dbg","name":"ExponentialUtilities.jl"
