by changing only the syntax of several lines I implemented the GPU support for the `expv` and `expv_timestep` functions. The following code works now

```julia
using LinearAlgebra
using SparseArrays
using ExponentialUtilities
using BenchmarkTools
using CUDA
using CUDA.CUSPARSE
CUDA.allowscalar(false)
##
n = 70000
A = sprand(ComplexF64, n, n, 10 / n)
A = triu(A, 1) + sprand(ComplexF64, n, n, 1 / n)
b = rand(ComplexF64, n)

t = 0.1
ts = Array(LinRange(0, 1, 300))

expv(t, A, b)
@btime expv_timestep(ts, A, b)

A = CuSparseMatrixCSR(A)
b = CuArray(b)

expv(t, A, b)

@btime expv_timestep(ts, A, b)
```","created_at":"2022-10-18T00:45:52Z","updated_at":"2022-10-18T08:58:36Z","closed_at":null,"merged_at":null,"merge_commit_sha":"ffbbdba1bb256b76ace9fff65a94ed782610f6e9","assignee":null,"assignees":[],"requested_reviewers":[],"requested_teams":[],"labels":[],"milestone":null,"draft":false,"commits_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/101/commits","review_comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/101/comments","review_comment_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/comments{/number}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/issues/101/comments","statuses_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/statuses/e91873dc4a02aabce2ac27314cc6b93fb5ad7dd3","head":{"label":"albertomercurio:master","ref":"master","sha":"e91873dc4a02aabce2ac27314cc6b93fb5ad7dd3","user":{"login":"albertomercurio","id":61953577,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYxOTUzNTc3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/61953577?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio","html_url":"https://github.com/albertomercurio","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"repo":{"id":540984174,"node_id":"R_kgDOID7Dbg","name":"ExponentialUtilities.jl" by changing only the syntax of several lines I implemented the GPU support for the `expv` and `expv_timestep` functions. The following code works now

```julia
using LinearAlgebra
using SparseArrays
using ExponentialUtilities
using BenchmarkTools
using CUDA
using CUDA.CUSPARSE
CUDA.allowscalar(false)
##
n = 70000
A = sprand(ComplexF64, n, n, 10 / n)
A = triu(A, 1) + sprand(ComplexF64, n, n, 1 / n)
b = rand(ComplexF64, n)

t = 0.1
ts = Array(LinRange(0, 1, 300))

expv(t, A, b)
@btime expv_timestep(ts, A, b)

A = CuSparseMatrixCSR(A)
b = CuArray(b)

expv(t, A, b)

@btime expv_timestep(ts, A, b)
```","created_at":"2022-10-18T00:45:52Z","updated_at":"2022-10-18T09:01:21Z","closed_at":null,"merged_at":null,"merge_commit_sha":"ffbbdba1bb256b76ace9fff65a94ed782610f6e9","assignee":null,"assignees":[],"requested_reviewers":[],"requested_teams":[],"labels":[],"milestone":null,"draft":false,"commits_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/101/commits","review_comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/101/comments","review_comment_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/comments{/number}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/issues/101/comments","statuses_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/statuses/e91873dc4a02aabce2ac27314cc6b93fb5ad7dd3","head":{"label":"albertomercurio:master","ref":"master","sha":"e91873dc4a02aabce2ac27314cc6b93fb5ad7dd3","user":{"login":"albertomercurio","id":61953577,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYxOTUzNTc3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/61953577?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio","html_url":"https://github.com/albertomercurio","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"repo":{"id":540984174,"node_id":"R_kgDOID7Dbg","name":"ExponentialUtilities.jl" by changing only the syntax of several lines I implemented the GPU support for the `expv` and `expv_timestep` functions. The following code works now

```julia
using LinearAlgebra
using SparseArrays
using ExponentialUtilities
using BenchmarkTools
using CUDA
using CUDA.CUSPARSE
CUDA.allowscalar(false)
##
n = 70000
A = sprand(ComplexF64, n, n, 10 / n)
A = triu(A, 1) + sprand(ComplexF64, n, n, 1 / n)
b = rand(ComplexF64, n)

t = 0.1
ts = Array(LinRange(0, 1, 300))

expv(t, A, b)
@btime expv_timestep(ts, A, b)

A = CuSparseMatrixCSR(A)
b = CuArray(b)

expv(t, A, b)

@btime expv_timestep(ts, A, b)
```","created_at":"2022-10-18T00:45:52Z","updated_at":"2022-10-18T09:01:21Z","closed_at":null,"merged_at":null,"merge_commit_sha":"ffbbdba1bb256b76ace9fff65a94ed782610f6e9","assignee":null,"assignees":[],"requested_reviewers":[],"requested_teams":[],"labels":[],"milestone":null,"draft":false,"commits_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/101/commits","review_comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/101/comments","review_comment_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/comments{/number}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/issues/101/comments","statuses_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/statuses/e91873dc4a02aabce2ac27314cc6b93fb5ad7dd3","head":{"label":"albertomercurio:master","ref":"master","sha":"e91873dc4a02aabce2ac27314cc6b93fb5ad7dd3","user":{"login":"albertomercurio","id":61953577,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYxOTUzNTc3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/61953577?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio","html_url":"https://github.com/albertomercurio","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"repo":{"id":540984174,"node_id":"R_kgDOID7Dbg","name":"ExponentialUtilities.jl" by changing only the syntax of several lines I implemented the GPU support for the `expv` and `expv_timestep` functions. The following code works now

```julia
using LinearAlgebra
using SparseArrays
using ExponentialUtilities
using BenchmarkTools
using CUDA
using CUDA.CUSPARSE
CUDA.allowscalar(false)
##
n = 70000
A = sprand(ComplexF64, n, n, 10 / n)
A = triu(A, 1) + sprand(ComplexF64, n, n, 1 / n)
b = rand(ComplexF64, n)

t = 0.1
ts = Array(LinRange(0, 1, 300))

expv(t, A, b)
@btime expv_timestep(ts, A, b)

A = CuSparseMatrixCSR(A)
b = CuArray(b)

expv(t, A, b)

@btime expv_timestep(ts, A, b)
```","created_at":"2022-10-18T00:45:52Z","updated_at":"2022-10-20T09:30:45Z","closed_at":null,"merged_at":null,"merge_commit_sha":"f0e139304b14c86277a32856bec3cfc5f5e94789","assignee":null,"assignees":[],"requested_reviewers":[],"requested_teams":[],"labels":[],"milestone":null,"draft":false,"commits_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/101/commits","review_comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/101/comments","review_comment_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/comments{/number}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/issues/101/comments","statuses_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/statuses/04727fc9b4cc751b53abb49931a8856caad17dbc","head":{"label":"albertomercurio:master","ref":"master","sha":"04727fc9b4cc751b53abb49931a8856caad17dbc","user":{"login":"albertomercurio","id":61953577,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYxOTUzNTc3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/61953577?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio","html_url":"https://github.com/albertomercurio","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"repo":{"id":540984174,"node_id":"R_kgDOID7Dbg","name":"ExponentialUtilities.jl" by changing only the syntax of several lines I implemented the GPU support for the `expv` and `expv_timestep` functions. The following code works now

```julia
using LinearAlgebra
using SparseArrays
using ExponentialUtilities
using BenchmarkTools
using CUDA
using CUDA.CUSPARSE
CUDA.allowscalar(false)
##
n = 70000
A = sprand(ComplexF64, n, n, 10 / n)
A = triu(A, 1) + sprand(ComplexF64, n, n, 1 / n)
b = rand(ComplexF64, n)

t = 0.1
ts = Array(LinRange(0, 1, 300))

expv(t, A, b)
@btime expv_timestep(ts, A, b)

A = CuSparseMatrixCSR(A)
b = CuArray(b)

expv(t, A, b)

@btime expv_timestep(ts, A, b)
```","created_at":"2022-10-18T00:45:52Z","updated_at":"2022-10-20T09:30:45Z","closed_at":null,"merged_at":null,"merge_commit_sha":"f0e139304b14c86277a32856bec3cfc5f5e94789","assignee":null,"assignees":[],"requested_reviewers":[],"requested_teams":[],"labels":[],"milestone":null,"draft":false,"commits_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/101/commits","review_comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/101/comments","review_comment_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/comments{/number}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/issues/101/comments","statuses_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/statuses/04727fc9b4cc751b53abb49931a8856caad17dbc","head":{"label":"albertomercurio:master","ref":"master","sha":"04727fc9b4cc751b53abb49931a8856caad17dbc","user":{"login":"albertomercurio","id":61953577,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYxOTUzNTc3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/61953577?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio","html_url":"https://github.com/albertomercurio","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"repo":{"id":540984174,"node_id":"R_kgDOID7Dbg","name":"ExponentialUtilities.jl" by changing only the syntax of several lines I implemented the GPU support for the `expv` and `expv_timestep` functions. The following code works now

```julia
using LinearAlgebra
using SparseArrays
using ExponentialUtilities
using BenchmarkTools
using CUDA
using CUDA.CUSPARSE
CUDA.allowscalar(false)
##
n = 70000
A = sprand(ComplexF64, n, n, 10 / n)
A = triu(A, 1) + sprand(ComplexF64, n, n, 1 / n)
b = rand(ComplexF64, n)

t = 0.1
ts = Array(LinRange(0, 1, 300))

expv(t, A, b)
@btime expv_timestep(ts, A, b)

A = CuSparseMatrixCSR(A)
b = CuArray(b)

expv(t, A, b)

@btime expv_timestep(ts, A, b)
```","created_at":"2022-10-18T00:45:52Z","updated_at":"2022-10-20T09:31:07Z","closed_at":null,"merged_at":null,"merge_commit_sha":"f0e139304b14c86277a32856bec3cfc5f5e94789","assignee":null,"assignees":[],"requested_reviewers":[],"requested_teams":[],"labels":[],"milestone":null,"draft":false,"commits_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/101/commits","review_comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/101/comments","review_comment_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/comments{/number}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/issues/101/comments","statuses_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/statuses/04727fc9b4cc751b53abb49931a8856caad17dbc","head":{"label":"albertomercurio:master","ref":"master","sha":"04727fc9b4cc751b53abb49931a8856caad17dbc","user":{"login":"albertomercurio","id":61953577,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYxOTUzNTc3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/61953577?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio","html_url":"https://github.com/albertomercurio","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"repo":{"id":540984174,"node_id":"R_kgDOID7Dbg","name":"ExponentialUtilities.jl" by changing only the syntax of several lines I implemented the GPU support for the `expv` and `expv_timestep` functions. The following code works now

```julia
using LinearAlgebra
using SparseArrays
using ExponentialUtilities
using BenchmarkTools
using CUDA
using CUDA.CUSPARSE
CUDA.allowscalar(false)
##
n = 70000
A = sprand(ComplexF64, n, n, 10 / n)
A = triu(A, 1) + sprand(ComplexF64, n, n, 1 / n)
b = rand(ComplexF64, n)

t = 0.1
ts = Array(LinRange(0, 1, 300))

expv(t, A, b)
@btime expv_timestep(ts, A, b)

A = CuSparseMatrixCSR(A)
b = CuArray(b)

expv(t, A, b)

@btime expv_timestep(ts, A, b)
```","created_at":"2022-10-18T00:45:52Z","updated_at":"2022-10-20T09:31:07Z","closed_at":null,"merged_at":null,"merge_commit_sha":"f0e139304b14c86277a32856bec3cfc5f5e94789","assignee":null,"assignees":[],"requested_reviewers":[],"requested_teams":[],"labels":[],"milestone":null,"draft":false,"commits_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/101/commits","review_comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/101/comments","review_comment_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pulls/comments{/number}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/issues/101/comments","statuses_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/statuses/04727fc9b4cc751b53abb49931a8856caad17dbc","head":{"label":"albertomercurio:master","ref":"master","sha":"04727fc9b4cc751b53abb49931a8856caad17dbc","user":{"login":"albertomercurio","id":61953577,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYxOTUzNTc3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/61953577?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio","html_url":"https://github.com/albertomercurio","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"repo":{"id":540984174,"node_id":"R_kgDOID7Dbg","name":"ExponentialUtilities.jl" by changing only the syntax of several lines I implemented the GPU support for the `expv` and `expv_timestep` functions. The following code works now

```julia
using LinearAlgebra
using SparseArrays
using ExponentialUtilities
using BenchmarkTools
using CUDA
using CUDA.CUSPARSE
CUDA.allowscalar(false)
##
n = 70000
A = sprand(ComplexF64, n, n, 10 / n)
A = triu(A, 1) + sprand(ComplexF64, n, n, 1 / n)
b = rand(ComplexF64, n)

t = 0.1
ts = Array(LinRange(0, 1, 300))

expv(t, A, b)
@btime expv_timestep(ts, A, b)

A = CuSparseMatrixCSR(A)
b = CuArray(b)

expv(t, A, b)

@btime expv_timestep(ts, A, b)
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/issues/101/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/issues/101/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/issues/comments/1287696675","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/pull/101#issuecomment-1287696675","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/issues/101","id":1287696675,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCJGC9s5MwLEj","user":{"login":"albertomercurio","id":61953577,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYxOTUzNTc3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/61953577?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio","html_url":"https://github.com/albertomercurio","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/albertomercurio/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-10-22T09:45:11Z","updated_at":"2022-10-22T09:45:11Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Ok I restored the old `copyto!` with the views. The problem was that, when trying to make compatible with GPU Arrays, I canged the PhivCache to be similar to the GPU Array. But then I restored it to be always a CPU array, since it includes only small matrices, like the Hessenberg matrix of the Krylov subspace.

Thus, now there is no more this problem, and the original `copyto!` is restored.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ExponentialUtilities.jl/issues/comments/1287696675/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

I always used the adaptive LinearExponential algorithm, but now does not work anymore.

The following code no more works

```julia
using LinearAlgebra
using SparseArrays
using DifferentialEquations

A = sprand(ComplexF64, 100, 100, 0.1)
A += A'
A = -1im * A

ψ0 = rand(eltype(A), size(A, 1))

t_l = LinRange(0, 10, 100)

tspan = (t_l[1], t_l[end])


A2 = DiffEqArrayOperator(A)
prob = ODEProblem(A2, ψ0, tspan)
sol = solve(prob, LinearExponential(krylov=:adaptive), dt = (t_l[2] - t_l[1]))
```

using ExponentialUtilities

expv_timestep(Array(t_l), A2, ψ0, adaptive=true)

I have a problem in implementing the Distributed ensemble in the case I use a remote host. The very simple code is

```julia
using Distributed
addprocs(6, restrict = false)
addprocs([("alberto@1.2.3.4", 16)], tunnel=true, dir = "/home/alberto", max_parallel = 64, enable_threaded_blas = true)

@everywhere using DifferentialEquations

@everywhere f(u,p,t) = 1.01*u
prob = ODEProblem(f,0.5,(0.0,1.0))
# prob = ODEProblem((u,p,t)->1.01u,0.5,(0.0,1.0))
@everywhere function prob_func(prob,i,repeat)
    remake(prob,u0=rand()*prob.u0)
end
ensemble_prob = EnsembleProblem(prob,prob_func=prob_func)
sim = solve(ensemble_prob,Tsit5(),EnsembleDistributed(),trajectories=100)
```

```julia

I'm interested on using these tools with CUDA Arrays like ComplexF64 dense CuArray or sparse CuSparseMatrix. Following the documentation, it only needs that the array supports the following functions:

- [x]  `Base.eltype(A)`
- [x]  `Base.size(A, dim)`
- [x]  `LinearAlgebra.mul!(y, A, x)`
- [ ]  `LinearAlgebra.opnorm(A, p=Inf)` (I can insert manually this value)
- [x]  `LinearAlgebra.ishermitian(A)`

To prove that this is the following code:
```julia
test = CUSPARSE.CuSparseMatrixCSC(sprand(ComplexF64, 100, 100, 0.01))
if Base.eltype(test) == ComplexF64
    println("OK")
end
if Base.size(test, 2) == 100
    println("OK")
end
dy = CUDA.zeros(ComplexF64, 100)
y = CUDA.rand(ComplexF64, 100)
LinearAlgebra.mul!(dy, test, y)
test2 = Array(test)
dy2 = Array(dy)
y2 = Array(y)
LinearAlgebra.mul!(dy2, test2, y2)
if Array(dy) == dy2
    println("OK")
end
```

But I can't calculate for example the function `expv` that it returns the error

```julia
expv(1.0, test, y, opnorm = opnorm(test2))

Scalar indexing is disallowed.
Invocation of getindex resulted in scalar indexing of a GPU array.
This is typically caused by calling an iterating implementation of a method.
Such implementations *do not* execute on the GPU, but very slowly on the CPU,
and therefore are only permitted from the REPL for prototyping purposes.
If you did intend to index this array, annotate the caller with @allowscalar.
```
